   186→def elkan_iter_chunked_dense(
   187→        const floating[:, ::1] X,                      # IN
   188→        const floating[::1] sample_weight,             # IN
   189→        const floating[:, ::1] centers_old,            # IN
   190→        floating[:, ::1] centers_new,                  # OUT
   191→        floating[::1] weight_in_clusters,              # OUT
   192→        const floating[:, ::1] center_half_distances,  # IN
   193→        const floating[::1] distance_next_center,      # IN
   194→        floating[::1] upper_bounds,                    # INOUT
   195→        floating[:, ::1] lower_bounds,                 # INOUT
   196→        int[::1] labels,                               # INOUT
   197→        floating[::1] center_shift,                    # OUT
   198→        int n_threads,
   199→        bint update_centers=True):
   200→    """Single iteration of K-means Elkan algorithm with dense input.
   201→
   202→    Update labels and centers (inplace), for one iteration, distributed
   203→    over data chunks.
   204→
   205→    Parameters
   206→    ----------
   207→    X : ndarray of shape (n_samples, n_features), dtype=floating
   208→        The observations to cluster.
   209→
   210→    sample_weight : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=floating
   211→        The weights for each observation in X.
   212→
   213→    centers_old : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features), dtype=floating
   214→        Centers before previous iteration, placeholder for the centers after
   215→        previous iteration.
   216→
   217→    centers_new : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features), dtype=floating
   218→        Centers after previous iteration, placeholder for the new centers
   219→        computed during this iteration.
   220→
   221→    weight_in_clusters : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,), dtype=floating
   222→        Placeholder for the sums of the weights of every observation assigned
   223→        to each center.
   224→
   225→    center_half_distances : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_clusters), \
   226→            dtype=floating
   227→        Half pairwise distances between centers.
   228→
   229→    distance_next_center : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,), dtype=floating
   230→        Distance between each center its closest center.
   231→
   232→    upper_bounds : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=floating
   233→        Upper bound for the distance between each sample and its center,
   234→        updated inplace.
   235→
   236→    lower_bounds : ndarray of shape (n_samples, n_clusters), dtype=floating
   237→        Lower bound for the distance between each sample and each center,
   238→        updated inplace.
   239→
   240→    labels : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=int
   241→        labels assignment.
   242→
   243→    center_shift : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,), dtype=floating
   244→        Distance between old and new centers.
   245→
   246→    n_threads : int
   247→        The number of threads to be used by openmp.
   248→
   249→    update_centers : bool
   250→        - If True, the labels and the new centers will be computed, i.e. runs
   251→          the E-step and the M-step of the algorithm.
   252→        - If False, only the labels will be computed, i.e runs the E-step of
   253→          the algorithm. This is useful especially when calling predict on a
   254→          fitted model.
   255→    """
   256→    cdef:
   257→        int n_samples = X.shape[0]
   258→        int n_features = X.shape[1]
   259→        int n_clusters = centers_new.shape[0]
   260→
   261→    if n_samples == 0:
   262→        # An empty array was passed, do nothing and return early (before
   263→        # attempting to compute n_chunks). This can typically happen when
   264→        # calling the prediction function of a bisecting k-means model with a
   265→        # large fraction of outliers.
   266→        return
   267→
   268→    cdef:
   269→        # hard-coded number of samples per chunk. Splitting in chunks is
   270→        # necessary to get parallelism. Chunk size chosen to be same as lloyd's
   271→        int n_samples_chunk = CHUNK_SIZE if n_samples > CHUNK_SIZE else n_samples
   272→        int n_chunks = n_samples // n_samples_chunk
   273→        int n_samples_rem = n_samples % n_samples_chunk
   274→        int chunk_idx
   275→        int start, end
   276→
   277→        int i, j, k
   278→
   279→        floating *centers_new_chunk
   280→        floating *weight_in_clusters_chunk
   281→
   282→        omp_lock_t lock
   283→
   284→    # count remainder chunk in total number of chunks
   285→    n_chunks += n_samples != n_chunks * n_samples_chunk
   286→
   287→    # number of threads should not be bigger than number of chunks
   288→    n_threads = min(n_threads, n_chunks)
   289→
   290→    if update_centers:
   291→        memset(&centers_new[0, 0], 0, n_clusters * n_features * sizeof(floating))
   292→        memset(&weight_in_clusters[0], 0, n_clusters * sizeof(floating))
   293→        omp_init_lock(&lock)
   294→
   295→    with nogil, parallel(num_threads=n_threads):
   296→        # thread local buffers
   297→        centers_new_chunk = <floating*> calloc(n_clusters * n_features, sizeof(floating))
   298→        weight_in_clusters_chunk = <floating*> calloc(n_clusters, sizeof(floating))
   299→
   300→        for chunk_idx in prange(n_chunks, schedule='static'):
   301→            start = chunk_idx * n_samples_chunk
   302→            if chunk_idx == n_chunks - 1 and n_samples_rem > 0:
   303→                end = start + n_samples_rem
   304→            else:
   305→                end = start + n_samples_chunk
   306→
   307→            _update_chunk_dense(
   308→                X[start: end],
   309→                sample_weight[start: end],
   310→                centers_old,
   311→                center_half_distances,
   312→                distance_next_center,
   313→                labels[start: end],
   314→                upper_bounds[start: end],
   315→                lower_bounds[start: end],
   316→                centers_new_chunk,
   317→                weight_in_clusters_chunk,
   318→                update_centers)
   319→
   320→        # reduction from local buffers.
   321→        if update_centers:
   322→            # The lock is necessary to avoid race conditions when aggregating
   323→            # info from different thread-local buffers.
   324→            omp_set_lock(&lock)
   325→            for j in range(n_clusters):
   326→                weight_in_clusters[j] += weight_in_clusters_chunk[j]
   327→                for k in range(n_features):
   328→                    centers_new[j, k] += centers_new_chunk[j * n_features + k]
   329→            omp_unset_lock(&lock)
   330→
   331→        free(centers_new_chunk)
   332→        free(weight_in_clusters_chunk)
   333→
   334→    if update_centers:
   335→        omp_destroy_lock(&lock)
   336→        _relocate_empty_clusters_dense(X, sample_weight, centers_old,
   337→                                       centers_new, weight_in_clusters, labels)
   338→
   339→        _average_centers(centers_new, weight_in_clusters)
   340→        _center_shift(centers_old, centers_new, center_shift)
   341→
   342→        # update lower and upper bounds
   343→        for i in range(n_samples):
   344→            upper_bounds[i] += center_shift[labels[i]]
   345→
   346→            for j in range(n_clusters):
   347→                lower_bounds[i, j] -= center_shift[j]
   348→                if lower_bounds[i, j] < 0:
   349→                    lower_bounds[i, j] = 0
   350→
   351→
   352→cdef void _update_chunk_dense(
   353→        const floating[:, ::1] X,                      # IN
   354→        const floating[::1] sample_weight,             # IN
   355→        const floating[:, ::1] centers_old,            # IN
   356→        const floating[:, ::1] center_half_distances,  # IN
   357→        const floating[::1] distance_next_center,      # IN
   358→        int[::1] labels,                               # INOUT
   359→        floating[::1] upper_bounds,                    # INOUT
   360→        floating[:, ::1] lower_bounds,                 # INOUT
   361→        floating *centers_new,                         # OUT
   362→        floating *weight_in_clusters,                  # OUT
   363→        bint update_centers) noexcept nogil:
   364→    """K-means combined EM step for one dense data chunk.
   365→
   366→    Compute the partial contribution of a single data chunk to the labels and
   367→    centers.
   368→    """
   369→    cdef:
   370→        int n_samples = labels.shape[0]
   371→        int n_clusters = centers_old.shape[0]
   372→        int n_features = centers_old.shape[1]
   373→
   374→        floating upper_bound, distance
   375→        int i, j, k, label
   376→
   377→    for i in range(n_samples):
   378→        upper_bound = upper_bounds[i]
   379→        bounds_tight = 0
   380→        label = labels[i]
   381→
   382→        # Next center is not far away from the currently assigned center.
   383→        # Sample might need to be assigned to another center.
   384→        if not distance_next_center[label] >= upper_bound:
   385→
   386→            for j in range(n_clusters):
   387→
   388→                # If this holds, then center_index is a good candidate for the
   389→                # sample to be relabelled, and we need to confirm this by
   390→                # recomputing the upper and lower bounds.
   391→                if (
   392→                    j != label
   393→                    and (upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j])
   394→                    and (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])
   395→                ):
   396→
   397→                    # Recompute upper bound by calculating the actual distance
   398→                    # between the sample and its current assigned center.
   399→                    if not bounds_tight:
   400→                        upper_bound = _euclidean_dense_dense(
   401→                            &X[i, 0], &centers_old[label, 0], n_features, False)
   402→                        lower_bounds[i, label] = upper_bound
   403→                        bounds_tight = 1
   404→
   405→                    # If the condition still holds, then compute the actual
   406→                    # distance between the sample and center. If this is less
   407→                    # than the previous distance, reassign label.
   408→                    if (
   409→                        upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j]
   410→                        or (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])
   411→                    ):
   412→
   413→                        distance = _euclidean_dense_dense(
   414→                            &X[i, 0], &centers_old[j, 0], n_features, False)
   415→                        lower_bounds[i, j] = distance
   416→                        if distance < upper_bound:
   417→                            label = j
   418→                            upper_bound = distance
   419→
   420→            labels[i] = label
   421→            upper_bounds[i] = upper_bound
   422→
   423→        if update_centers:
   424→            weight_in_clusters[label] += sample_weight[i]
   425→            for k in range(n_features):
   426→                centers_new[label * n_features + k] += X[i, k] * sample_weight[i]
   427→
   428→
   429→def elkan_iter_chunked_sparse(
   430→        X,                                             # IN
   431→        const floating[::1] sample_weight,             # IN
   432→        const floating[:, ::1] centers_old,            # IN
   433→        floating[:, ::1] centers_new,                  # OUT
   434→        floating[::1] weight_in_clusters,              # OUT
   435→        const floating[:, ::1] center_half_distances,  # IN
   436→        const floating[::1] distance_next_center,      # IN
   437→        floating[::1] upper_bounds,                    # INOUT
   438→        floating[:, ::1] lower_bounds,                 # INOUT
   439→        int[::1] labels,                               # INOUT
   440→        floating[::1] center_shift,                    # OUT
   441→        int n_threads,
   442→        bint update_centers=True):
   443→    """Single iteration of K-means Elkan algorithm with sparse input.
   444→
   445→    Update labels and centers (inplace), for one iteration, distributed
   446→    over data chunks.
   447→
   448→    Parameters
   449→    ----------
   450→    X : sparse matrix of shape (n_samples, n_features)
   451→        The observations to cluster. Must be in CSR format.
   452→
   453→    sample_weight : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=floating
   454→        The weights for each observation in X.
   455→
   456→    centers_old : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features), dtype=floating
   457→        Centers before previous iteration, placeholder for the centers after
   458→        previous iteration.
   459→
   460→    centers_new : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features), dtype=floating
   461→        Centers after previous iteration, placeholder for the new centers
   462→        computed during this iteration.
   463→
   464→    weight_in_clusters : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,), dtype=floating
   465→        Placeholder for the sums of the weights of every observation assigned
   466→        to each center.
   467→
   468→    center_half_distances : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_clusters), \
   469→            dtype=floating
   470→        Half pairwise distances between centers.
   471→
   472→    distance_next_center : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,), dtype=floating
   473→        Distance between each center its closest center.
   474→
   475→    upper_bounds : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=floating
   476→        Upper bound for the distance between each sample and its center,
   477→        updated inplace.
   478→
   479→    lower_bounds : ndarray of shape (n_samples, n_clusters), dtype=floating
   480→        Lower bound for the distance between each sample and each center,
   481→        updated inplace.
   482→
   483→    labels : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=int
   484→        labels assignment.
   485→
   486→    center_shift : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,), dtype=floating
   487→        Distance between old and new centers.
   488→
   489→    n_threads : int
   490→        The number of threads to be used by openmp.
   491→
   492→    update_centers : bool
   493→        - If True, the labels and the new centers will be computed, i.e. runs
   494→          the E-step and the M-step of the algorithm.
   495→        - If False, only the labels will be computed, i.e runs the E-step of
   496→          the algorithm. This is useful especially when calling predict on a
   497→          fitted model.
   498→    """
   499→    cdef:
   500→        int n_samples = X.shape[0]
   501→        int n_features = X.shape[1]
   502→        int n_clusters = centers_new.shape[0]
   503→
   504→    if n_samples == 0:
   505→        # An empty array was passed, do nothing and return early (before
   506→        # attempting to compute n_chunks). This can typically happen when
   507→        # calling the prediction function of a bisecting k-means model with a
   508→        # large fraction of outliers.
   509→        return
   510→
   511→    cdef:
   512→        floating[::1] X_data = X.data
   513→        int[::1] X_indices = X.indices
   514→        int[::1] X_indptr = X.indptr
   515→
   516→        # hard-coded number of samples per chunk. Splitting in chunks is
   517→        # necessary to get parallelism. Chunk size chosen to be same as lloyd's
   518→        int n_samples_chunk = CHUNK_SIZE if n_samples > CHUNK_SIZE else n_samples
   519→        int n_chunks = n_samples // n_samples_chunk
   520→        int n_samples_rem = n_samples % n_samples_chunk
   521→        int chunk_idx
   522→        int start, end
   523→
   524→        int i, j, k
   525→
   526→        floating[::1] centers_squared_norms = row_norms(centers_old, squared=True)
   527→
   528→        floating *centers_new_chunk
   529→        floating *weight_in_clusters_chunk
   530→
   531→        omp_lock_t lock
   532→
   533→    # count remainder chunk in total number of chunks
   534→    n_chunks += n_samples != n_chunks * n_samples_chunk
   535→
   536→    # number of threads should not be bigger than number of chunks
   537→    n_threads = min(n_threads, n_chunks)
   538→
   539→    if update_centers:
   540→        memset(&centers_new[0, 0], 0, n_clusters * n_features * sizeof(floating))
   541→        memset(&weight_in_clusters[0], 0, n_clusters * sizeof(floating))
   542→        omp_init_lock(&lock)
   543→
   544→    with nogil, parallel(num_threads=n_threads):
   545→        # thread local buffers
   546→        centers_new_chunk = <floating*> calloc(n_clusters * n_features, sizeof(floating))
   547→        weight_in_clusters_chunk = <floating*> calloc(n_clusters, sizeof(floating))
   548→
   549→        for chunk_idx in prange(n_chunks, schedule='static'):

This is useful especially when calling predict on a\n          fitted model.\n    \"\"\"\n    cdef:\n        int n_samples = X.shape[0]\n        int n_features = X.shape[1]\n        int n_clusters = centers_new.shape[0]\n\n    if n_samples == 0:\n        # An empty array was passed, do nothing and return early (before\n        # attempting to compute n_chunks). Splitting in chunks is\n        # necessary to get parallelism. Chunk size chosen to be same as lloyd's\n        int n_samples_chunk = CHUNK_SIZE if n_samples > CHUNK_SIZE else n_samples\n        int n_chunks = n_samples // n_samples_chunk\n        int n_samples_rem = n_samples % n_samples_chunk\n        int chunk_idx\n        int start, end\n\n        int i, j, k\n\n        floating[::1] centers_squared_norms = row_norms(centers_old, squared=True)\n\n        floating *centers_new_chunk\n        floating *weight_in_clusters_chunk\n\n        omp_lock_t lock\n\n    # count remainder chunk in total number of chunks\n    n_chunks += n_samples != n_chunks * n_samples_chunk\n\n    # number of threads should not be bigger than number of chunks\n    n_threads = min(n_threads, n_chunks)\n\n    if update_centers:\n        memset(&centers_new[0, 0], 0, n_clusters * n_features * sizeof(floating))\n        memset(&weight_in_clusters[0], 0, n_clusters * sizeof(floating))\n        omp_init_lock(&lock)\n\n    with nogil, parallel(num_threads=n_threads):\n        # thread local buffers\n        centers_new_chunk = <floating*> calloc(n_clusters * n_features, sizeof(floating))\n        weight_in_clusters_chunk = <floating*> calloc(n_clusters, sizeof(floating))\n\n        for chunk_idx in prange(n_chunks, schedule='static'):\n            start = chunk_idx * n_samples_chunk\n            if chunk_idx == n_chunks - 1 and n_samples_rem > 0:\n                end = start + n_samples_rem\n            else:\n                end = start + n_samples_chunk\n\n            _update_chunk_sparse(\n                X_data[X_indptr[start]: X_indptr[end]],\n                X_indices[X_indptr[start]: X_indptr[end]],\n                X_indptr[start: end+1],\n                sample_weight[start: end],\n                centers_old,\n                centers_squared_norms,\n                center_half_distances,\n                distance_next_center,\n                labels[start: end],\n                upper_bounds[start: end],\n                lower_bounds[start: end],\n                centers_new_chunk,\n                weight_in_clusters_chunk,\n                update_centers)\n\n        # reduction from local buffers.\n        if update_centers:\n            # The lock is necessary to avoid race conditions when aggregating\n            # info from different thread-local buffers.\n            omp_set_lock(&lock)\n            for j in range(n_clusters):\n                weight_in_clusters[j] += weight_in_clusters_chunk[j]\n                for k in range(n_features):\n                    centers_new[j, k] += centers_new_chunk[j * n_features + k]\n            omp_unset_lock(&lock)\n\n        free(centers_new_chunk)\n        free(weight_in_clusters_chunk)\n\n    if update_centers:\n        omp_destroy_lock(&lock)\n        _relocate_empty_clusters_sparse(\n            X_data, X_indices, X_indptr, sample_weight,\n            centers_old, centers_new, weight_in_clusters, labels)\n\n        _average_centers(centers_new, weight_in_clusters)\n        _center_shift(centers_old, centers_new, center_shift)\n\n        # IN\n        const int[::1] X_indices,                      # IN\n        const int[::1] X_indptr,                       # IN\n        const floating[::1] sample_weight,             # IN\n        const floating[:, ::1] centers_old,            # IN\n        const floating[::1] centers_squared_norms,     # IN\n        const floating[:, ::1] center_half_distances,  # IN\n        const floating[::1] distance_next_center,      # IN\n        int[::1] labels,                               # INOUT\n        floating[::1] upper_bounds,                    # INOUT\n        floating[:, ::1] lower_bounds,                 # INOUT\n        floating *centers_new,                         # OUT\n        floating *weight_in_clusters,                  # OUT\n        bint update_centers) noexcept nogil:\n    \"\"\"K-means combined EM step for one sparse data chunk.\n\n    Compute the partial contribution of a single data chunk to the labels and\n    centers.\n    \"\"\"\n    cdef:\n        int n_samples = labels.shape[0]\n        int n_clusters = centers_old.shape[0]\n        int n_features = centers_old.shape[1]\n\n        floating upper_bound, distance\n        int i, j, k, label\n        int s = X_indptr[0]\n\n    for i in range(n_samples):\n        upper_bound = upper_bounds[i]\n        bounds_tight = 0\n        label = labels[i]\n\n        # Next center is not far away from the currently assigned center.\n        # Sample might need to be assigned to another center.\n        if not distance_next_center[label] >= upper_bound:\n\n            for j in range(n_clusters):\n\n                # Recompute upper bound by calculating the actual distance\n                    # between the sample and its current assigned center.\n                    if not bounds_tight:\n                        upper_bound = _euclidean_sparse_dense(\n                            X_data[X_indptr[i] - s: X_indptr[i + 1] - s],\n                            X_indices[X_indptr[i] - s: X_indptr[i + 1] - s],\n                            centers_old[label], centers_squared_norms[label], False)\n                        lower_bounds[i, label] = upper_bound\n                        bounds_tight = 1\n\n                    #     54→# Initialization heuristic
    55→
    56→
    57→@validate_params(
    58→    {
    59→        "X": ["array-like", "sparse matrix"],
    60→        "n_clusters": [Interval(Integral, 1, None, closed="left")],
    61→        "sample_weight": ["array-like", None],
    62→        "x_squared_norms": ["array-like", None],
    63→        "random_state": ["random_state"],

    65→    },
    66→    prefer_skip_nested_validation=True,
    67→)
    68→def kmeans_plusplus(
    69→    X,
    70→    n_clusters,
    71→    *,
    72→    sample_weight=None,
    73→    x_squared_norms=None,
    74→    random_state=None,
    75→    n_local_trials=None,
    76→):
    77→    """Init n_clusters seeds according to k-means++.
    78→
    79→    .. versionadded:: 0.24
    80→
    81→    Parameters
    82→    ----------
    83→    X : {array-like, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
    84→        The data to pick seeds from.
    85→
    86→    n_clusters : int
    87→        The number of centroids to initialize.
    88→
    89→    sample_weight : array-like of shape (n_samples,), default=None
    90→        The weights for each observation in `X`. If `None`, all observations
    91→        are assigned equal weight. `sample_weight` is ignored if `init`
    92→        is a callable or a user provided array.
    93→
    94→        .. versionadded:: 1.3
    95→
    96→    x_squared_norms : array-like of shape (n_samples,), default=None
    97→        Squared Euclidean norm of each data point.
    98→
    99→    random_state : int or RandomState instance, default=None
   100→        Determines random number generation for centroid initialization. Pass
   101→        an int for reproducible output across multiple function calls.
   102→        See :term:`Glossary <random_state>`.
   103→
   104→    n_local_trials : int, default=None
   105→        The number of seeding trials for each center (except the first),
   106→        of which the one reducing inertia the most is greedily chosen.
   107→        Set to None to make the number of trials depend logarithmically
   108→        on the number of seeds (2+log(k)) which is the recommended setting.
   109→        Setting to 1 disables the greedy cluster selection and recovers the
   110→        vanilla k-means++ algorithm which was empirically shown to work less
   111→        well than its greedy variant.
   112→
   113→    Returns
   114→    -------
   115→    centers : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
   116→        The initial centers for k-means.
   117→
   118→    indices : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,)
   119→        The index location of the chosen centers in the data array X. For a
   120→        given index and center, X[index] = center.
   121→
   122→    Notes
   123→    -----
   124→    Selects initial cluster centers for k-mean clustering in a smart way
   125→    to speed up convergence. see: Arthur, D. and Vassilvitskii, S.
   126→    "k-means++: the advantages of careful seeding". ACM-SIAM symposium
   127→    on Discrete algorithms. 2007
   128→
   129→    Examples
   130→    --------
   131→
   132→    >>> from sklearn.cluster import kmeans_plusplus
   133→    >>> import numpy as np
   134→    >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [1, 4], [1, 0],
   135→    ...               [10, 2], [10, 4], [10, 0]])
   136→    >>> centers, indices = kmeans_plusplus(X, n_clusters=2, random_state=0)
   137→    >>> centers
   138→    array([[10,  2],
   139→           [ 1,  0]])
   140→    >>> indices
   141→    array([3, 2])
   142→    """
   143→    # Check data
   144→    check_array(X, accept_sparse="csr", dtype=[np.float64, np.float32])
   145→    sample_weight = _check_sample_weight(sample_weight, X, dtype=X.dtype)
   146→
   147→    if X.shape[0] < n_clusters:
   148→        raise ValueError(
   149→            f"n_samples={X.shape[0]} should be >= n_clusters={n_clusters}."
   150→        )
   151→
   152→    # Check parameters
   153→    if x_squared_norms is None:
   154→        x_squared_norms = row_norms(X, squared=True)
   155→    else:
   156→        x_squared_norms = check_array(x_squared_norms, dtype=X.dtype, ensure_2d=False)
   157→
   158→    if x_squared_norms.shape[0] != X.shape[0]:
   159→        raise ValueError(
   160→            f"The length of x_squared_norms {x_squared_norms.shape[0]} should "
   161→            f"be equal to the length of n_samples {X.shape[0]}."
   162→        )
   163→
   164→    random_state = check_random_state(random_state)
   165→
   166→    # Call private k-means++
   167→    centers, indices = _kmeans_plusplus(
   168→        X, n_clusters, x_squared_norms, sample_weight, random_state, n_local_trials
   169→    )
   170→
   171→    return centers, indices
   172→
   173→
   174→def _kmeans_plusplus(
   175→    X, n_clusters, x_squared_norms, sample_weight, random_state, n_local_trials=None
   176→):
   177→    """Computational component for initialization of n_clusters by
   178→    k-means++. Prior validation of data is assumed.
   179→
   180→    Parameters
   181→    ----------
   182→    X : {ndarray, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
   183→        The data to pick seeds for.
   184→
   185→    n_clusters : int
   186→        The number of seeds to choose.
   187→
   188→    sample_weight : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   189→        The weights for each observation in `X`.
   190→
   191→    x_squared_norms : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   192→        Squared Euclidean norm of each data point.
   193→
   194→    random_state : RandomState instance
   195→        The generator used to initialize the centers.
   196→        See :term:`Glossary <random_state>`.
   197→
   198→    n_local_trials : int, default=None
   199→        The number of seeding trials for each center (except the first),
   200→        of which the one reducing inertia the most is greedily chosen.
   201→        Set to None to make the number of trials depend logarithmically
   202→        on the number of seeds (2+log(k)); this is the default.
   203→
   204→    Returns
   205→    -------
   206→    centers : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
   207→        The initial centers for k-means.
   208→
   209→    indices : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,)
   210→        The index location of the chosen centers in the data array X. For a
   211→        given index and center, X[index] = center.
   212→    """
   213→    n_samples, n_features = X.shape
   214→
   215→    centers = np.empty((n_clusters, n_features), dtype=X.dtype)
   216→
   217→    # Set the number of local seeding trials if none is given
   218→    if n_local_trials is None:
   219→        # This is what Arthur/Vassilvitskii tried, but did not report
   220→        # specific results for other than mentioning in the conclusion
   221→        # that it helped.
   222→        n_local_trials = 2 + int(np.log(n_clusters))
   223→
   224→    # Pick first center randomly and track index of point
   225→    center_id = random_state.choice(n_samples, p=sample_weight / sample_weight.sum())
   226→    indices = np.full(n_clusters, -1, dtype=int)
   227→    if sp.issparse(X):
   228→        centers[0] = X[[center_id]].toarray()
   229→    else:
   230→        centers[0] = X[center_id]
   231→    indices[0] = center_id
   232→
   233→    # Initialize list of closest distances and calculate current potential
   234→    closest_dist_sq = _euclidean_distances(
   235→        centers[0, np.newaxis], X, Y_norm_squared=x_squared_norms, squared=True
   236→    )
   237→    current_pot = closest_dist_sq @ sample_weight
   238→
   239→    # Pick the remaining n_clusters-1 points
   240→    for c in range(1, n_clusters):
   241→        # Choose center candidates by sampling with probability proportional
   242→        # to the squared distance to the closest existing center
   243→        rand_vals = random_state.uniform(size=n_local_trials) * current_pot
   244→        candidate_ids = np.searchsorted(
   245→            stable_cumsum(sample_weight * closest_dist_sq), rand_vals
   246→        )
   247→        # XXX: numerical imprecision can result in a candidate_id out of range
   248→        np.clip(candidate_ids, None, closest_dist_sq.size - 1, out=candidate_ids)
   249→
   250→        # Compute distances to center candidates
   251→        distance_to_candidates = _euclidean_distances(
   252→            X[candidate_ids], X, Y_norm_squared=x_squared_norms, squared=True
   253→        )
   254→
   255→        # update closest distances squared and potential for each candidate
   256→        np.minimum(closest_dist_sq, distance_to_candidates, out=distance_to_candidates)
   257→        candidates_pot = distance_to_candidates @ sample_weight.reshape(-1, 1)
   258→
   259→        # Decide which candidate is the best
   260→        best_candidate = np.argmin(candidates_pot)
   261→        current_pot = candidates_pot[best_candidate]
   262→        closest_dist_sq = distance_to_candidates[best_candidate]
   263→        best_candidate = candidate_ids[best_candidate]
   264→
   265→        # Permanently add best center candidate found in local tries
   266→        if sp.issparse(X):
   267→            centers[c] = X[[best_candidate]].toarray()
   268→        else:
   269→            centers[c] = X[best_candidate]
   270→        indices[c] = best_candidate
   271→
   272→    return centers, indices
   273→
   274→
   275→###############################################################################
   276→# K-means batch estimation by EM (expectation maximization)
   277→
   278→
   279→def _tolerance(X, tol):
   280→    """Return a tolerance which is dependent on the dataset."""
   281→    if tol == 0:
   282→        return 0
   283→    if sp.issparse(X):
   284→        variances = mean_variance_axis(X, axis=0)[1]
   285→    else:
   286→        variances = np.var(X, axis=0)
   287→    return np.mean(variances) * tol
   288→
   289→
   290→@validate_params(
   291→    {
   292→        "X": ["array-like", "sparse matrix"],
   293→        "sample_weight": ["array-like", None],
   294→        "return_n_iter": [bool],
   295→    },
   296→    prefer_skip_nested_validation=False,
   297→)
   298→def k_means(
   299→    X,
   300→    n_clusters,
   301→    *,
   302→    sample_weight=None,

   305→    max_iter=300,
   306→    verbose=False,
   307→    tol=1e-4,
   308→    random_state=None,
   309→    copy_x=True,

   311→    return_n_iter=False,

   313→    """Perform K-means clustering algorithm.
   314→
   315→    Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <k_means>`.
   316→
   317→    Parameters
   318→    ----------
   319→    X : {array-like, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
   320→        The observations to cluster. It must be noted that the data
   321→        will be converted to C ordering, which will cause a memory copy
   322→        if the given data is not C-contiguous.
   323→
   324→    n_clusters : int
   325→        The number of clusters to form as well as the number of
   326→        centroids to generate.
   327→
   328→    sample_weight : array-like of shape (n_samples,), default=None
   329→        The weights for each observation in `X`. If `None`, all observations
   330→        are assigned equal weight. `sample_weight` is not used during
   331→        initialization if `init` is a callable or a user provided array.
   332→
   333→    init : {'k-means++', 'random'}, callable or array-like of shape \
   334→            (n_clusters, n_features), default='k-means++'
   335→        Method for initialization:
   336→
   337→        - `'k-means++'` : selects initial cluster centers for k-mean
   338→          clustering in a smart way to speed up convergence. See section
   339→          Notes in k_init for more details.
   340→        - `'random'`: choose `n_clusters` observations (rows) at random from data
   341→          for the initial centroids.
   342→        - If an array is passed, it should be of shape `(n_clusters, n_features)`
   343→          and gives the initial centers.
   344→        - If a callable is passed, it should take arguments `X`, `n_clusters` and a
   345→          random state and return an initialization.
   346→
   347→    n_init : 'auto' or int, default="auto"
   348→        Number of time the k-means algorithm will be run with different
   349→        centroid seeds. The final results will be the best output of
   350→        n_init consecutive runs in terms of inertia.
   351→
   352→        When `n_init='auto'`, the number of runs depends on the value of init:
   353→        10 if using `init='random'` or `init` is a callable;
   354→        1 if using `init='k-means++'` or `init` is an array-like.
   355→
   356→        .. versionadded:: 1.2
   357→           Added 'auto' option for `n_init`.
   358→
   359→        .. versionchanged:: 1.4
   360→           Default value for `n_init` changed to `'auto'`.
   361→
   362→    max_iter : int, default=300
   363→        Maximum number of iterations of the k-means algorithm to run.
   364→
   365→    verbose : bool, default=False
   366→        Verbosity mode.
   367→
   368→    tol : float, default=1e-4
   369→        Relative tolerance with regards to Frobenius norm of the difference
   370→        in the cluster centers of two consecutive iterations to declare
   371→        convergence.
   372→
   373→    random_state : int, RandomState instance or None, default=None
   374→        Determines random number generation for centroid initialization. Use
   375→        an int to make the randomness deterministic.
   376→        See :term:`Glossary <random_state>`.
   377→
   378→    copy_x : bool, default=True
   379→        When pre-computing distances it is more numerically accurate to center
   380→        the data first. If `copy_x` is True (default), then the original data is
   381→        not modified. If False, the original data is modified, and put back
   382→        before the function returns, but small numerical differences may be
   383→        introduced by subtracting and then adding the data mean. Note that if
   384→        the original data is not C-contiguous, a copy will be made even if
   385→        `copy_x` is False. If the original data is sparse, but not in CSR format,
   386→        a copy will be made even if `copy_x` is False.
   387→
   388→    algorithm : {"lloyd", "elkan"}, default="lloyd"
   389→        K-means algorithm to use. The classical EM-style algorithm is `"lloyd"`.
   390→        The `"elkan"` variation can be more efficient on some datasets with
   391→        well-defined clusters, by using the triangle inequality. However it's
   392→        more memory intensive due to the allocation of an extra array of shape
   393→        `(n_samples, n_clusters)`.
   394→
   395→        .. versionchanged:: 0.18
   396→            Added Elkan algorithm
   397→
   398→        .. versionchanged:: 1.1
   399→            Renamed "full" to "lloyd", and deprecated "auto" and "full".
   400→            Changed "auto" to use "lloyd" instead of "elkan".
   401→
   402→    return_n_iter : bool, default=False
   403→        Whether or not to return the number of iterations.
   404→
   405→    Returns
   406→    -------
   407→    centroid : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
   408→        Centroids found at the last iteration of k-means.
   409→
   410→    label : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   411→        The `label[i]` is the code or index of the centroid the
   412→        i'th observation is closest to.
   413→
   414→    inertia : float
   415→        The final value of the inertia criterion (sum of squared distances to
   416→        the closest centroid for all observations in the training set).
   417→
   418→    best_n_iter : int
   419→        Number of iterations corresponding to the best results.
   420→        Returned only if `return_n_iter` is set to True.
   421→
   422→    Examples
   423→    --------
   424→    >>> import numpy as np
   425→    >>> from sklearn.cluster import k_means
   426→    >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [1, 4], [1, 0],
   427→    ...               [10, 2], [10, 4], [10, 0]])
   428→    >>> centroid, label, inertia = k_means(
   429→    ...     X, n_clusters=2, n_init="auto", random_state=0
   430→    ... )
   431→    >>> centroid
   432→    array([[10.,  2.],
   433→           [ 1.,  2.]])
   434→    >>> label
   435→    array([1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0], dtype=int32)
   436→    >>> inertia
   437→    16.0
   438→    """
   439→    est = KMeans(
   440→        n_clusters=n_clusters,
   441→        init=init,
   442→        n_init=n_init,
   443→        max_iter=max_iter,
   444→        verbose=verbose,
   445→        tol=tol,
   446→        random_state=random_state,
   447→        copy_x=copy_x,
   448→        algorithm=algorithm,
   449→    ).fit(X, sample_weight=sample_weight)
   450→    if return_n_iter:
   451→        return est.cluster_centers_, est.labels_, est.inertia_, est.n_iter_
   452→    else:
   453→        return est.cluster_centers_, est.labels_, est.inertia_
   454→
   455→
   456→def _kmeans_single_elkan(
   457→    X,
   458→    sample_weight,
   459→    centers_init,
   460→    max_iter=300,
   461→    verbose=False,
   462→    tol=1e-4,
   463→    n_threads=1,
   464→):
   465→    """A single run of k-means elkan, assumes preparation completed prior.
   466→
   467→    Parameters
   468→    ----------
   469→    X : {ndarray, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
   470→        The observations to cluster. If sparse matrix, must be in CSR format.
   471→
   472→    sample_weight : array-like of shape (n_samples,)
   473→        The weights for each observation in X.
   474→
   475→    centers_init : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
   476→        The initial centers.
   477→
   478→    max_iter : int, default=300
   479→        Maximum number of iterations of the k-means algorithm to run.
   480→
   481→    verbose : bool, default=False
   482→        Verbosity mode.
   483→
   484→    tol : float, default=1e-4
   485→        Relative tolerance with regards to Frobenius norm of the difference
   486→        in the cluster centers of two consecutive iterations to declare
   487→        convergence.
   488→        It's not advised to set `tol=0` since convergence might never be
   489→        declared due to rounding errors. Use a very small number instead.
   490→
   491→    n_threads : int, default=1
   492→        The number of OpenMP threads to use for the computation. Parallelism is
   493→        sample-wise on the main cython loop which assigns each sample to its
   494→        closest center.
   495→
   496→    Returns
   497→    -------
   498→    centroid : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
   499→        Centroids found at the last iteration of k-means.
   500→
   501→    label : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   502→        label[i] is the code or index of the centroid the
   503→        i'th observation is closest to.
   504→
   505→    inertia : float
   506→        The final value of the inertia criterion (sum of squared distances to
   507→        the closest centroid for all observations in the training set).
   508→
   509→    n_iter : int
   510→        Number of iterations run.
   511→    """
   512→    n_samples = X.shape[0]
   513→    n_clusters = centers_init.shape[0]
   514→
   515→    # Buffers to avoid new allocations at each iteration.
   516→    centers = centers_init
   517→    centers_new = np.zeros_like(centers)
   518→    weight_in_clusters = np.zeros(n_clusters, dtype=X.dtype)
   519→    labels = np.full(n_samples, -1, dtype=np.int32)
   520→    labels_old = labels.copy()
   521→    center_half_distances = euclidean_distances(centers) / 2
   522→    distance_next_center = np.partition(
   523→        np.asarray(center_half_distances), kth=1, axis=0
   524→    )[1]
   525→    upper_bounds = np.zeros(n_samples, dtype=X.dtype)
   526→    lower_bounds = np.zeros((n_samples, n_clusters), dtype=X.dtype)
   527→    center_shift = np.zeros(n_clusters, dtype=X.dtype)
   528→
   529→    if sp.issparse(X):
   530→        init_bounds = init_bounds_sparse
   531→        elkan_iter = elkan_iter_chunked_sparse
   532→        _inertia = _inertia_sparse
   533→    else:
   534→        init_bounds = init_bounds_dense
   535→        elkan_iter = elkan_iter_chunked_dense
   536→        _inertia = _inertia_dense
   537→
   538→    init_bounds(
   539→        X,
   540→        centers,
   541→        center_half_distances,
   542→        labels,
   543→        upper_bounds,
   544→        lower_bounds,
   545→        n_threads=n_threads,
   546→    )
   547→
   548→    strict_convergence = False
   549→
   550→    for i in range(max_iter):
   551→        elkan_iter(
   552→            X,
   553→            sample_weight,
   554→            centers,
   555→            centers_new,
   556→            weight_in_clusters,
   557→            center_half_distances,
   558→            distance_next_center,
   559→            upper_bounds,
   560→            lower_bounds,
   561→            labels,
   562→            center_shift,
   563→            n_threads,
   564→        )
   565→
   566→        # compute new pairwise distances between centers and closest other
   567→        # center of each center for next iterations
   568→        center_half_distances = euclidean_distances(centers_new) / 2
   569→        distance_next_center = np.partition(
   570→            np.asarray(center_half_distances), kth=1, axis=0
   571→        )[1]
   572→
   573→        if verbose:
   574→            inertia = _inertia(X, sample_weight, centers, labels, n_threads)
   575→            print(f"Iteration {i}, inertia {inertia}")
   576→
   577→        centers, centers_new = centers_new, centers
   578→
   579→        if np.array_equal(labels, labels_old):
   580→            # First check the labels for strict convergence.
   581→            if verbose:
   582→                print(f"Converged at iteration {i}: strict convergence.")
   583→            strict_convergence = True
   584→            break
   585→        else:
   586→            # No strict convergence, check for tol based convergence.
   587→            center_shift_tot = (center_shift**2).sum()
   588→            if center_shift_tot <= tol:
   589→                if verbose:
   590→                    print(
   591→                        f"Converged at iteration {i}: center shift "
   592→                        f"{center_shift_tot} within tolerance {tol}."
   593→                    )
   594→                break
   595→
   596→        labels_old[:] = labels
   597→
   598→    if not strict_convergence:
   599→        # rerun E-step so that predicted labels match cluster centers
   600→        elkan_iter(
   601→            X,
   602→            sample_weight,
   603→            centers,
   604→            centers,
   605→            weight_in_clusters,
   606→            center_half_distances,
   607→            distance_next_center,
   608→            upper_bounds,
   609→            lower_bounds,
   610→            labels,
   611→            center_shift,
   612→            n_threads,
   613→            update_centers=False,
   614→        )
   615→
   616→    inertia = _inertia(X, sample_weight, centers, labels, n_threads)
   617→
   618→    return labels, inertia, centers, i + 1
   619→
   620→
   621→# Threadpoolctl context to limit the number of threads in second level of
   622→# nested parallelism (i.e. BLAS) to avoid oversubscription.
   623→@_threadpool_controller_decorator(limits=1, user_api="blas")
   624→def _kmeans_single_lloyd(
   625→    X,
   626→    sample_weight,
   627→    centers_init,
   628→    max_iter=300,
   629→    verbose=False,
   630→    tol=1e-4,
   631→    n_threads=1,
   632→):
   633→    """A single run of k-means lloyd, assumes preparation completed prior.
   634→
   635→    Parameters
   636→    ----------
   637→    X : {ndarray, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
   638→        The observations to cluster. If sparse matrix, must be in CSR format.
   639→
   640→    sample_weight : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   641→        The weights for each observation in X.
   642→
   643→    centers_init : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
   644→        The initial centers.
   645→
   646→    max_iter : int, default=300
   647→        Maximum number of iterations of the k-means algorithm to run.
   648→
   649→    verbose : bool, default=False
   650→        Verbosity mode
   651→
   652→    tol : float, default=1e-4
   653→        Relative tolerance with regards to Frobenius norm of the difference
   654→        in the cluster centers of two consecutive iterations to declare
   655→        convergence.
   656→        It's not advised to set `tol=0` since convergence might never be
   657→        declared due to rounding errors. Use a very small number instead.
   658→
   659→    n_threads : int, default=1
   660→        The number of OpenMP threads to use for the computation. Parallelism is
   661→        sample-wise on the main cython loop which assigns each sample to its
   662→        closest center.
   663→
   664→    Returns
   665→    -------
   666→    centroid : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
   667→        Centroids found at the last iteration of k-means.
   668→
   669→    label : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   670→        label[i] is the code or index of the centroid the
   671→        i'th observation is closest to.
   672→
   673→    inertia : float
   674→        The final value of the inertia criterion (sum of squared distances to
   675→        the closest centroid for all observations in the training set).
   676→
   677→    n_iter : int
   678→        Number of iterations run.
   679→    """
   680→    n_clusters = centers_init.shape[0]
   681→
   682→    # Buffers to avoid new allocations at each iteration.
   683→    centers = centers_init
   684→    centers_new = np.zeros_like(centers)
   685→    labels = np.full(X.shape[0], -1, dtype=np.int32)
   686→    labels_old = labels.copy()
   687→    weight_in_clusters = np.zeros(n_clusters, dtype=X.dtype)
   688→    center_shift = np.zeros(n_clusters, dtype=X.dtype)
   689→
   690→    if sp.issparse(X):
   691→        lloyd_iter = lloyd_iter_chunked_sparse
   692→        _inertia = _inertia_sparse
   693→    else:
   694→        lloyd_iter = lloyd_iter_chunked_dense
   695→        _inertia = _inertia_dense
   696→
   697→    strict_convergence = False
   698→
   699→    for i in range(max_iter):
   700→        lloyd_iter(
   701→            X,
   702→            sample_weight,
   703→            centers,
   704→            centers_new,
   705→            weight_in_clusters,
   706→            labels,
   707→            center_shift,
   708→            n_threads,
   709→        )
   710→
   711→        if verbose:
   712→            inertia = _inertia(X, sample_weight, centers, labels, n_threads)
   713→            print(f"Iteration {i}, inertia {inertia}.")
   714→
   715→        centers, centers_new = centers_new, centers
   716→
   717→        if np.array_equal(labels, labels_old):
   718→            # First check the labels for strict convergence.
   719→            if verbose:
   720→                print(f"Converged at iteration {i}: strict convergence.")
   721→            strict_convergence = True
   722→            break
   723→        else:
   724→            # No strict convergence, check for tol based convergence.
   725→            center_shift_tot = (center_shift**2).sum()
   726→            if center_shift_tot <= tol:
   727→                if verbose:
   728→                    print(
   729→                        f"Converged at iteration {i}: center shift "
   730→                        f"{center_shift_tot} within tolerance {tol}."
   731→                    )
   732→                break
   733→
   734→        labels_old[:] = labels
   735→
   736→    if not strict_convergence:
   737→        # rerun E-step so that predicted labels match cluster centers
   738→        lloyd_iter(
   739→            X,
   740→            sample_weight,
   741→            centers,
   742→            centers,
   743→            weight_in_clusters,
   744→            labels,
   745→            center_shift,
   746→            n_threads,
   747→            update_centers=False,
   748→        )
   749→
   750→    inertia = _inertia(X, sample_weight, centers, labels, n_threads)
   751→
   752→    return labels, inertia, centers, i + 1

   754→
   755→def _labels_inertia(X, sample_weight, centers, n_threads=1, return_inertia=True):
   756→    """E step of the K-means EM algorithm.
   757→
   758→    Compute the labels and the inertia of the given samples and centers.
   759→
   760→    Parameters
   761→    ----------
   762→    X : {ndarray, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
   763→        The input samples to assign to the labels. If sparse matrix, must
   764→        be in CSR format.
   765→
   766→    sample_weight : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   767→        The weights for each observation in X.
   768→
   769→    x_squared_norms : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   770→        Precomputed squared euclidean norm of each data point, to speed up
   771→        computations.
   772→
   773→    centers : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
   774→        The cluster centers.
   775→
   776→    n_threads : int, default=1
   777→        The number of OpenMP threads to use for the computation. Parallelism is
   778→        sample-wise on the main cython loop which assigns each sample to its
   779→        closest center.
   780→
   781→    return_inertia : bool, default=True
   782→        Whether to compute and return the inertia.
   783→
   784→    Returns
   785→    -------
   786→    labels : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   787→        The resulting assignment.
   788→
   789→    inertia : float
   790→        Sum of squared distances of samples to their closest cluster center.
   791→        Inertia is only returned if return_inertia is True.
   792→    """
   793→    n_samples = X.shape[0]
   794→    n_clusters = centers.shape[0]
   795→
   796→    labels = np.full(n_samples, -1, dtype=np.int32)
   797→    center_shift = np.zeros(n_clusters, dtype=centers.dtype)
   798→
   799→    if sp.issparse(X):
   800→        _labels = lloyd_iter_chunked_sparse
   801→        _inertia = _inertia_sparse
   802→    else:
   803→        _labels = lloyd_iter_chunked_dense
   804→        _inertia = _inertia_dense
   805→
   806→    _labels(
   807→        X,
   808→        sample_weight,
   809→        centers,
   810→        centers_new=None,
   811→        weight_in_clusters=None,
   812→        labels=labels,
   813→        center_shift=center_shift,
   814→        n_threads=n_threads,
   815→        update_centers=False,
   816→    )
   817→
   818→    if return_inertia:
   819→        inertia = _inertia(X, sample_weight, centers, labels, n_threads)
   820→        return labels, inertia
   821→
   822→    return labels
   823→
   824→
   825→# Same as _labels_inertia but in a threadpool_limits context.
   826→_labels_inertia_threadpool_limit = _threadpool_controller_decorator(
   827→    limits=1, user_api="blas"
   828→)(_labels_inertia)

   830→
   831→class _BaseKMeans(
   832→    ClassNamePrefixFeaturesOutMixin, TransformerMixin, ClusterMixin, BaseEstimator, ABC
   833→):
   834→    """Base class for KMeans and MiniBatchKMeans"""
   835→
   836→    _parameter_constraints: dict = {
   837→        "n_clusters": [Interval(Integral, 1, None, closed="left")],
   838→        "init": [StrOptions({"k-means++", "random"}), callable, "array-like"],
   839→        "n_init": [
   840→            StrOptions({"auto"}),
   841→            Interval(Integral, 1, None, closed="left"),
   842→        ],
   843→        "max_iter": [Interval(Integral, 1, None, closed="left")],
   844→        "tol": [Interval(Real, 0, None, closed="left")],
   845→        "verbose": ["verbose"],
   846→        "random_state": ["random_state"],
   847→    }
   848→
   849→    def __init__(
   850→        self,
   851→        n_clusters,
   852→        *,
   853→        init,
   854→        n_init,
   855→        max_iter,
   856→        tol,
   857→        verbose,
   858→        random_state,
   859→    ):
   860→        self.n_clusters = n_clusters
   861→        self.init = init
   862→        self.max_iter = max_iter
   863→        self.tol = tol
   864→        self.n_init = n_init
   865→        self.verbose = verbose
   866→        self.random_state = random_state
   867→
   868→    def _check_params_vs_input(self, X, default_n_init=None):
   869→        # n_clusters
   870→        if X.shape[0] < self.n_clusters:
   871→            raise ValueError(
   872→                f"n_samples={X.shape[0]} should be >= n_clusters={self.n_clusters}."
   873→            )
   874→
   875→        # tol
   876→        self._tol = _tolerance(X, self.tol)
   877→
   878→        # n-init
   879→        if self.n_init == "auto":
   880→            if isinstance(self.init, str) and self.init == "k-means++":
   881→                self._n_init = 1
   882→            elif isinstance(self.init, str) and self.init == "random":
   883→                self._n_init = default_n_init
   884→            elif callable(self.init):
   885→                self._n_init = default_n_init
   886→            else:  # array-like
   887→                self._n_init = 1
   888→        else:
   889→            self._n_init = self.n_init
   890→
   891→        if _is_arraylike_not_scalar(self.init) and self._n_init != 1:
   892→            warnings.warn(
   893→                (
   894→                    "Explicit initial center position passed: performing only"
   895→                    f" one init in {self.__class__.__name__} instead of "
   896→                    f"n_init={self._n_init}."
   897→                ),
   898→                RuntimeWarning,
   899→                stacklevel=2,
   900→            )
   901→            self._n_init = 1
   902→
   903→    @abstractmethod
   904→    def _warn_mkl_vcomp(self, n_active_threads):
   905→        """Issue an estimator specific warning when vcomp and mkl are both present
   906→
   907→        This method is called by `_check_mkl_vcomp`.
   908→        """
   909→
   910→    def _check_mkl_vcomp(self, X, n_samples):
   911→        """Check when vcomp and mkl are both present"""
   912→        # The BLAS call inside a prange in lloyd_iter_chunked_dense is known to
   913→        # cause a small memory leak when there are less chunks than the number
   914→        # of available threads. It only happens when the OpenMP library is
   915→        # vcomp (microsoft OpenMP) and the BLAS library is MKL. see #18653
   916→        if sp.issparse(X):
   917→            return
   918→
   919→        n_active_threads = int(np.ceil(n_samples / CHUNK_SIZE))
   920→        if n_active_threads < self._n_threads:
   921→            modules = _get_threadpool_controller().info()
   922→            has_vcomp = "vcomp" in [module["prefix"] for module in modules]
   923→            has_mkl = ("mkl", "intel") in [
   924→                (module["internal_api"], module.get("threading_layer", None))
   925→                for module in modules
   926→            ]
   927→            if has_vcomp and has_mkl:
   928→                self._warn_mkl_vcomp(n_active_threads)
   929→
   930→    def _validate_center_shape(self, X, centers):
   931→        """Check if centers is compatible with X and n_clusters."""
   932→        if centers.shape[0] != self.n_clusters:
   933→            raise ValueError(
   934→                f"The shape of the initial centers {centers.shape} does not "
   935→                f"match the number of clusters {self.n_clusters}."
   936→            )
   937→        if centers.shape[1] != X.shape[1]:
   938→            raise ValueError(
   939→                f"The shape of the initial centers {centers.shape} does not "
   940→                f"match the number of features of the data {X.shape[1]}."
   941→            )
   942→
   943→    def _check_test_data(self, X):
   944→        X = validate_data(
   945→            self,
   946→            X,
   947→            accept_sparse="csr",
   948→            reset=False,
   949→            dtype=[np.float64, np.float32],
   950→            order="C",
   951→            accept_large_sparse=False,
   952→        )
   953→        return X
   954→
   955→    def _init_centroids(
   956→        self,
   957→        X,
   958→        x_squared_norms,
   959→        init,
   960→        random_state,
   961→        sample_weight,
   962→        init_size=None,
   963→        n_centroids=None,
   964→    ):
   965→        """Compute the initial centroids.
   966→
   967→        Parameters
   968→        ----------
   969→        X : {ndarray, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
   970→            The input samples.
   971→
   972→        x_squared_norms : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   973→            Squared euclidean norm of each data point. Pass it if you have it
   974→            at hands already to avoid it being recomputed here.
   975→
   976→        init : {'k-means++', 'random'}, callable or ndarray of shape \
   977→                (n_clusters, n_features)
   978→            Method for initialization.
   979→
   980→        random_state : RandomState instance
   981→            Determines random number generation for centroid initialization.
   982→            See :term:`Glossary <random_state>`.
   983→
   984→        sample_weight : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
   985→            The weights for each observation in X. `sample_weight` is not used
   986→            during initialization if `init` is a callable or a user provided
   987→            array.
   988→
   989→        init_size : int, default=None
   990→            Number of samples to randomly sample for speeding up the
   991→            initialization (sometimes at the expense of accuracy).
   992→
   993→        n_centroids : int, default=None
   994→            Number of centroids to initialize.
   995→            If left to 'None' the number of centroids will be equal to
   996→            number of clusters to form (self.n_clusters).
   997→
   998→        Returns
   999→        -------
  1000→        centers : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
  1001→            Initial centroids of clusters.
  1002→        """
  1003→        n_samples = X.shape[0]
  1004→        n_clusters = self.n_clusters if n_centroids is None else n_centroids
  1005→
  1006→        if init_size is not None and init_size < n_samples:
  1007→            init_indices = random_state.randint(0, n_samples, init_size)
  1008→            X = X[init_indices]
  1009→            x_squared_norms = x_squared_norms[init_indices]
  1010→            n_samples = X.shape[0]
  1011→            sample_weight = sample_weight[init_indices]
  1012→
  1013→        if isinstance(init, str) and init == "k-means++":
  1014→            centers, _ = _kmeans_plusplus(
  1015→                X,
  1016→                n_clusters,
  1017→                random_state=random_state,
  1018→                x_squared_norms=x_squared_norms,
  1019→                sample_weight=sample_weight,
  1020→            )
  1021→        elif isinstance(init, str) and init == "random":
  1022→            seeds = random_state.choice(
  1023→                n_samples,
  1024→                size=n_clusters,
  1025→                replace=False,
  1026→                p=sample_weight / sample_weight.sum(),
  1027→            )
  1028→            centers = X[seeds]
  1029→        elif _is_arraylike_not_scalar(self.init):
  1030→            centers = init
  1031→        elif callable(init):
  1032→            centers = init(X, n_clusters, random_state=random_state)
  1033→            centers = check_array(centers, dtype=X.dtype, copy=False, order="C")
  1034→            self._validate_center_shape(X, centers)
  1035→
  1036→        if sp.issparse(centers):
  1037→            centers = centers.toarray()
  1038→
  1039→        return centers
  1040→
  1041→    def fit_predict(self, X, y=None, sample_weight=None):
  1042→        """Compute cluster centers and predict cluster index for each sample.
  1043→
  1044→        Convenience method; equivalent to calling fit(X) followed by
  1045→        predict(X).
  1046→
  1047→        Parameters
  1048→        ----------
  1049→        X : {array-like, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
  1050→            New data to transform.
  1051→
  1052→        y : Ignored
  1053→            Not used, present here for API consistency by convention.
  1054→
  1055→        sample_weight : array-like of shape (n_samples,), default=None
  1056→            The weights for each observation in X. If None, all observations
  1057→            are assigned equal weight.
  1058→
  1059→        Returns
  1060→        -------
  1061→        labels : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
  1062→            Index of the cluster each sample belongs to.
  1063→        """
  1064→        return self.fit(X, sample_weight=sample_weight).labels_
  1065→
  1066→    def predict(self, X):
  1067→        """Predict the closest cluster each sample in X belongs to.
  1068→
  1069→        In the vector quantization literature, `cluster_centers_` is called
  1070→        the code book and each value returned by `predict` is the index of
  1071→        the closest code in the code book.
  1072→
  1073→        Parameters
  1074→        ----------
  1075→        X : {array-like, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
  1076→            New data to predict.
  1077→
  1078→        Returns
  1079→        -------
  1080→        labels : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
  1081→            Index of the cluster each sample belongs to.
  1082→        """
  1083→        check_is_fitted(self)
  1084→
  1085→        X = self._check_test_data(X)
  1086→
  1087→        # sample weights are not used by predict but cython helpers expect an array
  1088→        sample_weight = np.ones(X.shape[0], dtype=X.dtype)
  1089→
  1090→        labels = _labels_inertia_threadpool_limit(
  1091→            X,
  1092→            sample_weight,
  1093→            self.cluster_centers_,
  1094→            n_threads=self._n_threads,
  1095→            return_inertia=False,
  1096→        )
  1097→
  1098→        return labels
  1099→
  1100→    def fit_transform(self, X, y=None, sample_weight=None):
  1101→        """Compute clustering and transform X to cluster-distance space.
  1102→
  1103→        Equivalent to fit(X).transform(X), but more efficiently implemented.
  1104→
  1105→        Parameters
  1106→        ----------
  1107→        X : {array-like, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
  1108→            New data to transform.
  1109→
  1110→        y : Ignored
  1111→            Not used, present here for API consistency by convention.
  1112→
  1113→        sample_weight : array-like of shape (n_samples,), default=None
  1114→            The weights for each observation in X. If None, all observations
  1115→            are assigned equal weight.
  1116→
  1117→        Returns
  1118→        -------
  1119→        X_new : ndarray of shape (n_samples, n_clusters)
  1120→            X transformed in the new space.
  1121→        """
  1122→        return self.fit(X, sample_weight=sample_weight)._transform(X)
  1123→
  1124→    def transform(self, X):
  1125→        """Transform X to a cluster-distance space.
  1126→
  1127→        In the new space, each dimension is the distance to the cluster
  1128→        centers. Note that even if X is sparse, the array returned by
  1129→        `transform` will typically be dense.
  1130→
  1131→        Parameters
  1132→        ----------
  1133→        X : {array-like, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
  1134→            New data to transform.
  1135→
  1136→        Returns
  1137→        -------
  1138→        X_new : ndarray of shape (n_samples, n_clusters)
  1139→            X transformed in the new space.
  1140→        """
  1141→        check_is_fitted(self)
  1142→
  1143→        X = self._check_test_data(X)
  1144→        return self._transform(X)
  1145→
  1146→    def _transform(self, X):
  1147→        """Guts of transform method; no input validation."""
  1148→        return euclidean_distances(X, self.cluster_centers_)
  1149→
  1150→    def score(self, X, y=None, sample_weight=None):
  1151→        """Opposite of the value of X on the K-means objective.
  1152→
  1153→        Parameters
  1154→        ----------
  1155→        X : {array-like, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
  1156→            New data.
  1157→
  1158→        y : Ignored
  1159→            Not used, present here for API consistency by convention.
  1160→
  1161→        sample_weight : array-like of shape (n_samples,), default=None
  1162→            The weights for each observation in X. If None, all observations
  1163→            are assigned equal weight.
  1164→
  1165→        Returns
  1166→        -------
  1167→        score : float
  1168→            Opposite of the value of X on the K-means objective.
  1169→        """
  1170→        check_is_fitted(self)
  1171→
  1172→        X = self._check_test_data(X)
  1173→        sample_weight = _check_sample_weight(sample_weight, X, dtype=X.dtype)
  1174→
  1175→        _, scores = _labels_inertia_threadpool_limit(
  1176→            X, sample_weight, self.cluster_centers_, self._n_threads
  1177→        )
  1178→        return -scores
  1179→
  1180→    def __sklearn_tags__(self):
  1181→        tags = super().__sklearn_tags__()
  1182→        tags.input_tags.sparse = True
  1183→        return tags
