  1186→class KMeans(_BaseKMeans):
  1187→    """K-Means clustering.
  1188→
  1189→    Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <k_means>`.
  1190→
  1191→    Parameters
  1192→    ----------
  1193→
  1194→    n_clusters : int, default=8
  1195→        The number of clusters to form as well as the number of
  1196→        centroids to generate.
  1197→
  1198→        For an example of how to choose an optimal value for `n_clusters` refer to
  1199→        :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_cluster_plot_kmeans_silhouette_analysis.py`.
  1200→
  1201→    init : {'k-means++', 'random'}, callable or array-like of shape \
  1202→            (n_clusters, n_features), default='k-means++'
  1203→        Method for initialization:
  1204→
  1205→        * 'k-means++' : selects initial cluster centroids using sampling \
  1206→            based on an empirical probability distribution of the points' \
  1207→            contribution to the overall inertia. This technique speeds up \
  1208→            convergence. The algorithm implemented is "greedy k-means++". It \
  1209→            differs from the vanilla k-means++ by making several trials at \
  1210→            each sampling step and choosing the best centroid among them.
  1211→
  1212→        * 'random': choose `n_clusters` observations (rows) at random from \
  1213→        data for the initial centroids.
  1214→
  1215→        * If an array is passed, it should be of shape (n_clusters, n_features)\
  1216→        and gives the initial centers.
  1217→
  1218→        * If a callable is passed, it should take arguments X, n_clusters and a\
  1219→        random state and return an initialization.
  1220→
  1221→        For an example of how to use the different `init` strategies, see
  1222→        :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_cluster_plot_kmeans_digits.py`.
  1223→
  1224→        For an evaluation of the impact of initialization, see the example
  1225→        :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_cluster_plot_kmeans_stability_low_dim_dense.py`.
  1226→
  1227→    n_init : 'auto' or int, default='auto'
  1228→        Number of times the k-means algorithm is run with different centroid
  1229→        seeds. The final results is the best output of `n_init` consecutive runs
  1230→        in terms of inertia. Several runs are recommended for sparse
  1231→        high-dimensional problems (see :ref:`kmeans_sparse_high_dim`).
  1232→
  1233→        When `n_init='auto'`, the number of runs depends on the value of init:
  1234→        10 if using `init='random'` or `init` is a callable;
  1235→        1 if using `init='k-means++'` or `init` is an array-like.
  1236→
  1237→        .. versionadded:: 1.2
  1238→           Added 'auto' option for `n_init`.
  1239→
  1240→        .. versionchanged:: 1.4
  1241→           Default value for `n_init` changed to `'auto'`.
  1242→
  1243→    max_iter : int, default=300
  1244→        Maximum number of iterations of the k-means algorithm for a
  1245→        single run.
  1246→
  1247→    tol : float, default=1e-4
  1248→        Relative tolerance with regards to Frobenius norm of the difference
  1249→        in the cluster centers of two consecutive iterations to declare
  1250→        convergence.
  1251→
  1252→    verbose : int, default=0
  1253→        Verbosity mode.
  1254→
  1255→    random_state : int, RandomState instance or None, default=None
  1256→        Determines random number generation for centroid initialization. Use
  1257→        an int to make the randomness deterministic.
  1258→        See :term:`Glossary <random_state>`.
  1259→
  1260→    copy_x : bool, default=True
  1261→        When pre-computing distances it is more numerically accurate to center
  1262→        the data first. If copy_x is True (default), then the original data is
  1263→        not modified. If False, the original data is modified, and put back
  1264→        before the function returns, but small numerical differences may be
  1265→        introduced by subtracting and then adding the data mean. Note that if
  1266→        the original data is not C-contiguous, a copy will be made even if
  1267→        copy_x is False. If the original data is sparse, but not in CSR format,
  1268→        a copy will be made even if copy_x is False.
  1269→
  1270→    algorithm : {"lloyd", "elkan"}, default="lloyd"
  1271→        K-means algorithm to use. The classical EM-style algorithm is `"lloyd"`.
  1272→        The `"elkan"` variation can be more efficient on some datasets with
  1273→        well-defined clusters, by using the triangle inequality. However it's
  1274→        more memory intensive due to the allocation of an extra array of shape
  1275→        `(n_samples, n_clusters)`.
  1276→
  1277→        .. versionchanged:: 0.18
  1278→            Added Elkan algorithm
  1279→
  1280→        .. versionchanged:: 1.1
  1281→            Renamed "full" to "lloyd", and deprecated "auto" and "full".
  1282→            Changed "auto" to use "lloyd" instead of "elkan".
  1283→
  1284→    Attributes
  1285→    ----------
  1286→    cluster_centers_ : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
  1287→        Coordinates of cluster centers. If the algorithm stops before fully
  1288→        converging (see ``tol`` and ``max_iter``), these will not be
  1289→        consistent with ``labels_``.
  1290→
  1291→    labels_ : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
  1292→        Labels of each point
  1293→
  1294→    inertia_ : float
  1295→        Sum of squared distances of samples to their closest cluster center,
  1296→        weighted by the sample weights if provided.
  1297→
  1298→    n_iter_ : int
  1299→        Number of iterations run.
  1300→
  1301→    n_features_in_ : int
  1302→        Number of features seen during :term:`fit`.
  1303→
  1304→        .. versionadded:: 0.24
  1305→
  1306→    feature_names_in_ : ndarray of shape (`n_features_in_`,)
  1307→        Names of features seen during :term:`fit`. Defined only when `X`
  1308→        has feature names that are all strings.
  1309→
  1310→        .. versionadded:: 1.0
  1311→
  1312→    See Also
  1313→    --------
  1314→    MiniBatchKMeans : Alternative online implementation that does incremental
  1315→        updates of the centers positions using mini-batches.
  1316→        For large scale learning (say n_samples > 10k) MiniBatchKMeans is
  1317→        probably much faster than the default batch implementation.
  1318→
  1319→    Notes
  1320→    -----
  1321→    The k-means problem is solved using either Lloyd's or Elkan's algorithm.
  1322→
  1323→    The average complexity is given by O(k n T), where n is the number of
  1324→    samples and T is the number of iteration.
  1325→
  1326→    The worst case complexity is given by O(n^(k+2/p)) with
  1327→    n = n_samples, p = n_features.
  1328→    Refer to :doi:`"How slow is the k-means method?" D. Arthur and S. Vassilvitskii -
  1329→    SoCG2006.<10.1145/1137856.1137880>` for more details.
  1330→
  1331→    In practice, the k-means algorithm is very fast (one of the fastest
  1332→    clustering algorithms available), but it falls in local minima. That's why
  1333→    it can be useful to restart it several times.
  1334→
  1335→    If the algorithm stops before fully converging (because of ``tol`` or
  1336→    ``max_iter``), ``labels_`` and ``cluster_centers_`` will not be consistent,
  1337→    i.e. the ``cluster_centers_`` will not be the means of the points in each
  1338→    cluster. Also, the estimator will reassign ``labels_`` after the last
  1339→    iteration to make ``labels_`` consistent with ``predict`` on the training
  1340→    set.
  1341→
  1342→    Examples
  1343→    --------
  1344→
  1345→    >>> from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
  1346→    >>> import numpy as np
  1347→    >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [1, 4], [1, 0],
  1348→    ...               [10, 2], [10, 4], [10, 0]])
  1349→    >>> kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=2, random_state=0, n_init="auto").fit(X)
  1350→    >>> kmeans.labels_
  1351→    array([1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0], dtype=int32)
  1352→    >>> kmeans.predict([[0, 0], [12, 3]])
  1353→    array([1, 0], dtype=int32)
  1354→    >>> kmeans.cluster_centers_
  1355→    array([[10.,  2.],
  1356→           [ 1.,  2.]])
  1357→
  1358→    For examples of common problems with K-Means and how to address them see
  1359→    :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_cluster_plot_kmeans_assumptions.py`.
  1360→
  1361→    For a demonstration of how K-Means can be used to cluster text documents see
  1362→    :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_text_plot_document_clustering.py`.
  1363→
  1364→    For a comparison between K-Means and MiniBatchKMeans refer to example
  1365→    :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_cluster_plot_mini_batch_kmeans.py`.
  1366→
  1367→    For a comparison between K-Means and BisectingKMeans refer to example
  1368→    :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_cluster_plot_bisect_kmeans.py`.
  1369→    """
  1370→
  1371→    _parameter_constraints: dict = {
  1372→        **_BaseKMeans._parameter_constraints,
  1373→        "copy_x": ["boolean"],
  1374→        "algorithm": [StrOptions({"lloyd", "elkan"})],
  1375→    }
  1376→
  1377→    def __init__(
  1378→        self,
  1379→        n_clusters=8,
  1380→        *,
  1381→        init="k-means++",
  1382→        n_init="auto",
  1383→        max_iter=300,
  1384→        tol=1e-4,
  1385→        verbose=0,
  1386→        random_state=None,
  1387→        copy_x=True,
  1388→        algorithm="lloyd",
  1389→    ):
  1390→        super().__init__(
  1391→            n_clusters=n_clusters,
  1392→            init=init,
  1393→            n_init=n_init,
  1394→            max_iter=max_iter,
  1395→            tol=tol,
  1396→            verbose=verbose,
  1397→            random_state=random_state,
  1398→        )
  1399→
  1400→        self.copy_x = copy_x
  1401→        self.algorithm = algorithm
  1402→
  1403→    def _check_params_vs_input(self, X):
  1404→        super()._check_params_vs_input(X, default_n_init=10)
  1405→
  1406→        self._algorithm = self.algorithm
  1407→        if self._algorithm == "elkan" and self.n_clusters == 1:
  1408→            warnings.warn(
  1409→                (
  1410→                    "algorithm='elkan' doesn't make sense for a single "
  1411→                    "cluster. Using 'lloyd' instead."
  1412→                ),
  1413→                RuntimeWarning,
  1414→            )
  1415→            self._algorithm = "lloyd"
  1416→
  1417→    def _warn_mkl_vcomp(self, n_active_threads):
  1418→        """Warn when vcomp and mkl are both present"""
  1419→        warnings.warn(
  1420→            "KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows "
  1421→            "with MKL, when there are less chunks than available "
  1422→            "threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment"
  1423→            f" variable OMP_NUM_THREADS={n_active_threads}."
  1424→        )
  1425→
  1426→    @_fit_context(prefer_skip_nested_validation=True)
  1427→    def fit(self, X, y=None, sample_weight=None):
  1428→        """Compute k-means clustering.
  1429→
  1430→        Parameters
  1431→        ----------
  1432→        X : {array-like, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
  1433→            Training instances to cluster. It must be noted that the data
  1434→            will be converted to C ordering, which will cause a memory
  1435→            copy if the given data is not C-contiguous.
  1436→            If a sparse matrix is passed, a copy will be made if it's not in
  1437→            CSR format.
  1438→
  1439→        y : Ignored
  1440→            Not used, present here for API consistency by convention.
  1441→
  1442→        sample_weight : array-like of shape (n_samples,), default=None
  1443→            The weights for each observation in X. If None, all observations
  1444→            are assigned equal weight. `sample_weight` is not used during
  1445→            initialization if `init` is a callable or a user provided array.
  1446→
  1447→            .. versionadded:: 0.20
  1448→
  1449→        Returns
  1450→        -------
  1451→        self : object
  1452→            Fitted estimator.
  1453→        """
  1454→        X = validate_data(
  1455→            self,
  1456→            X,
  1457→            accept_sparse="csr",
  1458→            dtype=[np.float64, np.float32],
  1459→            order="C",
  1460→            copy=self.copy_x,
  1461→            accept_large_sparse=False,
  1462→        )
  1463→
  1464→        self._check_params_vs_input(X)
  1465→
  1466→        random_state = check_random_state(self.random_state)
  1467→        sample_weight = _check_sample_weight(sample_weight, X, dtype=X.dtype)
  1468→        self._n_threads = _openmp_effective_n_threads()
  1469→
  1470→        # Validate init array
  1471→        init = self.init
  1472→        init_is_array_like = _is_arraylike_not_scalar(init)
  1473→        if init_is_array_like:
  1474→            init = check_array(init, dtype=X.dtype, copy=True, order="C")
  1475→            self._validate_center_shape(X, init)
  1476→
  1477→        # subtract of mean of x for more accurate distance computations
  1478→        if not sp.issparse(X):
  1479→            X_mean = X.mean(axis=0)
  1480→            # The copy was already done above
  1481→            X -= X_mean
  1482→
  1483→            if init_is_array_like:
  1484→                init -= X_mean
  1485→
  1486→        # precompute squared norms of data points
  1487→        x_squared_norms = row_norms(X, squared=True)
  1488→
  1489→        if self._algorithm == "elkan":
  1490→            kmeans_single = _kmeans_single_elkan
  1491→        else:
  1492→            kmeans_single = _kmeans_single_lloyd
  1493→            self._check_mkl_vcomp(X, X.shape[0])
  1494→
  1495→        best_inertia, best_labels = None, None
  1496→
  1497→        for i in range(self._n_init):
  1498→            # Initialize centers
  1499→            centers_init = self._init_centroids(
  1500→                X,
  1501→                x_squared_norms=x_squared_norms,
  1502→                init=init,
  1503→                random_state=random_state,
  1504→                sample_weight=sample_weight,
  1505→            )
  1506→            if self.verbose:
  1507→                print("Initialization complete")
  1508→
  1509→            # run a k-means once
  1510→            labels, inertia, centers, n_iter_ = kmeans_single(
  1511→                X,
  1512→                sample_weight,
  1513→                centers_init,
  1514→                max_iter=self.max_iter,
  1515→                verbose=self.verbose,
  1516→                tol=self._tol,
  1517→                n_threads=self._n_threads,
  1518→            )
  1519→
  1520→            # determine if these results are the best so far
  1521→            # we chose a new run if it has a better inertia and the clustering is
  1522→            # different from the best so far (it's possible that the inertia is
  1523→            # slightly better even if the clustering is the same with potentially
  1524→            # permuted labels, due to rounding errors)
  1525→            if best_inertia is None or (
  1526→                inertia < best_inertia
  1527→                and not _is_same_clustering(labels, best_labels, self.n_clusters)
  1528→            ):
  1529→                best_labels = labels
  1530→                best_centers = centers
  1531→                best_inertia = inertia
  1532→                best_n_iter = n_iter_
  1533→
  1534→        if not sp.issparse(X):
  1535→            if not self.copy_x:
  1536→                X += X_mean
  1537→            best_centers += X_mean
  1538→
  1539→        distinct_clusters = len(set(best_labels))
  1540→        if distinct_clusters < self.n_clusters:
  1541→            warnings.warn(
  1542→                "Number of distinct clusters ({}) found smaller than "
  1543→                "n_clusters ({}). Possibly due to duplicate points "
  1544→                "in X.".format(distinct_clusters, self.n_clusters),
  1545→                ConvergenceWarning,
  1546→                stacklevel=2,
  1547→            )
  1548→
  1549→        self.cluster_centers_ = best_centers
  1550→        self._n_features_out = self.cluster_centers_.shape[0]
  1551→        self.labels_ = best_labels
  1552→        self.inertia_ = best_inertia
  1553→        self.n_iter_ = best_n_iter
  1554→        return self
  1555→
  1556→
  1557→def _mini_batch_step(
  1558→    X,
  1559→    sample_weight,
  1560→    centers,
  1561→    centers_new,
  1562→    weight_sums,
  1563→    random_state,
  1564→    random_reassign=False,
  1565→    reassignment_ratio=0.01,
  1566→    verbose=False,
  1567→    n_threads=1,
  1568→):
  1569→    """Incremental update of the centers for the Minibatch K-Means algorithm.
  1570→
  1571→    Parameters
  1572→    ----------
  1573→
  1574→    X : {ndarray, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)
  1575→        The original data array. If sparse, must be in CSR format.
  1576→
  1577→    x_squared_norms : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
  1578→        Squared euclidean norm of each data point.
  1579→
  1580→    sample_weight : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
  1581→        The weights for each observation in `X`.
  1582→
  1583→    centers : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
  1584→        The cluster centers before the current iteration
  1585→
  1586→    centers_new : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
  1587→        The cluster centers after the current iteration. Modified in-place.
  1588→
  1589→    weight_sums : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,)
  1590→        The vector in which we keep track of the numbers of points in a
  1591→        cluster. This array is modified in place.
  1592→
  1593→    random_state : RandomState instance
  1594→        Determines random number generation for low count centers reassignment.
  1595→        See :term:`Glossary <random_state>`.
  1596→
  1597→    random_reassign : boolean, default=False
  1598→        If True, centers with very low counts are randomly reassigned
  1599→        to observations.
  1600→
  1601→    reassignment_ratio : float, default=0.01
  1602→        Control the fraction of the maximum number of counts for a
  1603→        center to be reassigned. A higher value means that low count
  1604→        centers are more likely to be reassigned, which means that the
  1605→        model will take longer to converge, but should converge in a
  1606→        better clustering.
  1607→
  1608→    verbose : bool, default=False
  1609→        Controls the verbosity.
  1610→
  1611→    n_threads : int, default=1
  1612→        The number of OpenMP threads to use for the computation.
  1613→
  1614→    Returns
  1615→    -------
  1616→    inertia : float
  1617→        Sum of squared distances of samples to their closest cluster center.
  1618→        The inertia is computed after finding the labels and before updating
  1619→        the centers.
  1620→    """
  1621→    # Perform label assignment to nearest centers
  1622→    # For better efficiency, it's better to run _mini_batch_step in a
  1623→    # threadpool_limit context than using _labels_inertia_threadpool_limit here
  1624→    labels, inertia = _labels_inertia(X, sample_weight, centers, n_threads=n_threads)
  1625→
  1626→    # Update centers according to the labels
  1627→    if sp.issparse(X):
  1628→        _minibatch_update_sparse(
  1629→            X, sample_weight, centers, centers_new, weight_sums, labels, n_threads
  1630→        )
  1631→    else:
  1632→        _minibatch_update_dense(
  1633→            X,
  1634→            sample_weight,
  1635→            centers,
  1636→            centers_new,
  1637→            weight_sums,
  1638→            labels,
  1639→            n_threads,
  1640→        )
  1641→
  1642→    # Reassign clusters that have very low weight
  1643→    if random_reassign and reassignment_ratio > 0:
  1644→        to_reassign = weight_sums < reassignment_ratio * weight_sums.max()
  1645→
  1646→        # pick at most .5 * batch_size samples as new centers
  1647→        if to_reassign.sum() > 0.5 * X.shape[0]:
  1648→            indices_dont_reassign = np.argsort(weight_sums)[int(0.5 * X.shape[0]) :]
  1649→            to_reassign[indices_dont_reassign] = False
  1650→        n_reassigns = to_reassign.sum()
  1651→
  1652→        if n_reassigns:
  1653→            # Pick new clusters amongst observations with uniform probability
  1654→            new_centers = random_state.choice(
  1655→                X.shape[0], replace=False, size=n_reassigns
  1656→            )
  1657→            if verbose:
  1658→                print(f"[MiniBatchKMeans] Reassigning {n_reassigns} cluster centers.")
  1659→
  1660→            if sp.issparse(X):
  1661→                assign_rows_csr(
  1662→                    X,
  1663→                    new_centers.astype(np.intp, copy=False),
  1664→                    np.where(to_reassign)[0].astype(np.intp, copy=False),
  1665→                    centers_new,
  1666→                )
  1667→            else:
  1668→                centers_new[to_reassign] = X[new_centers]
  1669→
  1670→        # reset counts of reassigned centers, but don't reset them too small
  1671→        # to avoid instant reassignment. This is a pretty dirty hack as it
  1672→        # also modifies the learning rates.
  1673→        weight_sums[to_reassign] = np.min(weight_sums[~to_reassign])
  1674→
  1675→    return inertia
  1676→
  1677→
  1678→class MiniBatchKMeans(_BaseKMeans):
  1679→    """
  1680→    Mini-Batch K-Means clustering.
  1681→
  1682→    Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <mini_batch_kmeans>`.
  1683→
  1684→    Parameters
  1685→    ----------
  1686→
  1687→    n_clusters : int, default=8
  1688→        The number of clusters to form as well as the number of
  1689→        centroids to generate.
  1690→
  1691→    init : {'k-means++', 'random'}, callable or array-like of shape \
  1692→            (n_clusters, n_features), default='k-means++'
  1693→        Method for initialization:
  1694→
  1695→        'k-means++' : selects initial cluster centroids using sampling based on
  1696→        an empirical probability distribution of the points' contribution to the
  1697→        overall inertia. This technique speeds up convergence. The algorithm
  1698→        implemented is "greedy k-means++". It differs from the vanilla k-means++
  1699→        by making several trials at each sampling step and choosing the best centroid
  1700→        among them.
  1701→
  1702→        'random': choose `n_clusters` observations (rows) at random from data
  1703→        for the initial centroids.
  1704→
  1705→        If an array is passed, it should be of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
  1706→        and gives the initial centers.
  1707→
  1708→        If a callable is passed, it should take arguments X, n_clusters and a
  1709→        random state and return an initialization.
  1710→
  1711→        For an evaluation of the impact of initialization, see the example
  1712→        :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_cluster_plot_kmeans_stability_low_dim_dense.py`.
  1713→
  1714→    max_iter : int, default=100
  1715→        Maximum number of iterations over the complete dataset before
  1716→        stopping independently of any early stopping criterion heuristics.
  1717→
  1718→    batch_size : int, default=1024
  1719→        Size of the mini batches.
  1720→        For faster computations, you can set the ``batch_size`` greater than
  1721→        256 * number of cores to enable parallelism on all cores.
  1722→
  1723→        .. versionchanged:: 1.0
  1724→           `batch_size` default changed from 100 to 1024.
  1725→
  1726→    verbose : int, default=0
  1727→        Verbosity mode.
  1728→
  1729→    compute_labels : bool, default=True
  1730→        Compute label assignment and inertia for the complete dataset
  1731→        once the minibatch optimization has converged in fit.
  1732→
  1733→    random_state : int, RandomState instance or None, default=None
  1734→        Determines random number generation for centroid initialization and
  1735→        random reassignment. Use an int to make the randomness deterministic.
  1736→        See :term:`Glossary <random_state>`.
  1737→
  1738→    tol : float, default=0.0
  1739→        Control early stopping based on the relative center changes as
  1740→        measured by a smoothed, variance-normalized of the mean center
  1741→        squared position changes. This early stopping heuristics is
  1742→        closer to the one used for the batch variant of the algorithms
  1743→        but induces a slight computational and memory overhead over the
  1744→        inertia heuristic.
  1745→
  1746→        To disable convergence detection based on normalized center
  1747→        change, set tol to 0.0 (default).
  1748→
  1749→    max_no_improvement : int, default=10
  1750→        Control early stopping based on the consecutive number of mini
  1751→        batches that does not yield an improvement on the smoothed inertia.
  1752→
  1753→        To disable convergence detection based on inertia, set
  1754→        max_no_improvement to None.
  1755→
  1756→    init_size : int, default=None
  1757→        Number of samples to randomly sample for speeding up the
  1758→        initialization (sometimes at the expense of accuracy): the
  1759→        only algorithm is initialized by running a batch KMeans on a
  1760→        random subset of the data. This needs to be larger than n_clusters.
  1761→
  1762→        If `None`, the heuristic is `init_size = 3 * batch_size` if
  1763→        `3 * batch_size < n_clusters`, else `init_size = 3 * n_clusters`.
  1764→
  1765→    n_init : 'auto' or int, default="auto"
  1766→        Number of random initializations that are tried.
  1767→        In contrast to KMeans, the algorithm is only run once, using the best of
  1768→        the `n_init` initializations as measured by inertia. Several runs are
  1769→        recommended for sparse high-dimensional problems (see
  1770→        :ref:`kmeans_sparse_high_dim`).
  1771→
  1772→        When `n_init='auto'`, the number of runs depends on the value of init:
  1773→        3 if using `init='random'` or `init` is a callable;
  1774→        1 if using `init='k-means++'` or `init` is an array-like.
  1775→
  1776→        .. versionadded:: 1.2
  1777→           Added 'auto' option for `n_init`.
  1778→
  1779→        .. versionchanged:: 1.4
  1780→           Default value for `n_init` changed to `'auto'` in version.
  1781→
  1782→    reassignment_ratio : float, default=0.01
  1783→        Control the fraction of the maximum number of counts for a center to
  1784→        be reassigned. A higher value means that low count centers are more
  1785→        easily reassigned, which means that the model will take longer to
  1786→        converge, but should converge in a better clustering. However, too high
  1787→        a value may cause convergence issues, especially with a small batch
  1788→        size.
  1789→
  1790→    Attributes
  1791→    ----------
  1792→
  1793→    cluster_centers_ : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)
  1794→        Coordinates of cluster centers.
  1795→
  1796→    labels_ : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)
  1797→        Labels of each point (if compute_labels is set to True).
  1798→
  1799→    inertia_ : float
  1800→        The value of the inertia criterion associated with the chosen
  1801→        partition if compute_labels is set to True. If compute_labels is set to
  1802→        False, it's an approximation of the inertia based on an exponentially
  1803→        weighted average of the batch inertiae.
  1804→        The inertia is defined as the sum of square distances of samples to
  1805→        their cluster center, weighted by the sample weights if provided.
  1806→
  1807→    n_iter_ : int
  1808→        Number of iterations over the full dataset.
  1809→
  1810→    n_steps_ : int
  1811→        Number of minibatches processed.
  1812→
  1813→        .. versionadded:: 1.0
  1814→
  1815→    n_features_in_ : int
  1816→        Number of features seen during :term:`fit`.
  1817→
  1818→        .. versionadded:: 0.24
  1819→
  1820→    feature_names_in_ : ndarray of shape (`n_features_in_`,)
  1821→        Names of features seen during :term:`fit`. Defined only when `X`
  1822→        has feature names that are all strings.
  1823→
  1824→        .. versionadded:: 1.0
  1825→
  1826→    See Also
  1827→    --------
  1828→    KMeans : The classic implementation of the clustering method based on the
  1829→        Lloyd's algorithm. It consumes the whole set of input data at each
  1830→        iteration.
  1831→
  1832→    Notes
  1833→    -----
  1834→    See https://www.eecs.tufts.edu/~dsculley/papers/fastkmeans.pdf
  1835→
  1836→    When there are too few points in the dataset, some centers may be
  1837→    duplicated, which means that a proper clustering in terms of the number
  1838→    of requesting clusters and the number of returned clusters will not
  1839→    always match. One solution is to set `reassignment_ratio=0`, which
  1840→    prevents reassignments of clusters that are too small.
  1841→
  1842→    See :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_cluster_plot_birch_vs_minibatchkmeans.py` for a
  1843→    comparison with :class:`~sklearn.cluster.BIRCH`.
  1844→
  1845→    Examples
  1846→    --------
  1847→    >>> from sklearn.cluster import MiniBatchKMeans
  1848→    >>> import numpy as np
  1849→    >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [1, 4], [1, 0],
  1850→    ...               [4, 2], [4, 0], [4, 4],
  1851→    ...               [4, 5], [0, 1], [2, 2],
  1852→    ...               [3, 2], [5, 5], [1, -1]])
  1853→    >>> # manually fit on batches
  1854→    >>> kmeans = MiniBatchKMeans(n_clusters=2,
  1855→    ...                          random_state=0,
  1856→    ...                          batch_size=6,
  1857→    ...                          n_init="auto")
  1858→    >>> kmeans = kmeans.partial_fit(X[0:6,:])
  1859→    >>> kmeans = kmeans.partial_fit(X[6:12,:])
  1860→    >>> kmeans.cluster_centers_
  1861→    array([[3.375, 3.  ],
  1862→           [0.75 , 0.5 ]])
  1863→    >>> kmeans.predict([[0, 0], [4, 4]])
  1864→    array([1, 0], dtype=int32)
  1865→    >>> # fit on the whole data
  1866→    >>> kmeans = MiniBatchKMeans(n_clusters=2,
  1867→    ...                          random_state=0,
  1868→    ...                          batch_size=6,
  1869→    ...                          max_iter=10,
  1870→    ...                          n_init="auto").fit(X)
  1871→    >>> kmeans.cluster_centers_
  1872→    array([[3.55102041, 2.48979592],
  1873→           [1.06896552, 1.        ]])
  1874→    >>> kmeans.predict([[0, 0], [4, 4]])
  1875→    array([1, 0], dtype=int32)
  1876→
  1877→    For a comparison of Mini-Batch K-Means clustering with other clustering algorithms,
  1878→    see :ref:`sphx_glr_auto_examples_cluster_plot_cluster_comparison.py`
  1879→    """
  1880→
  1881→    _parameter_constraints: dict = {
  1882→        **_BaseKMeans._parameter_constraints,
  1883→        "batch_size": [Interval(Integral, 1, None, closed="left")],
  1884→        "compute_labels": ["boolean"],
  1885→        "max_no_improvement": [Interval(Integral, 0, None, closed="left"), None],
  1886→        "init_size": [Interval(Integral, 1, None, closed="left"), None],
  1887→        "reassignment_ratio": [Interval(Real, 0, None, closed="left")],
  1888→    }
  1889→
  1890→    def __init__(
  1891→        self,
  1892→        n_clusters=8,
  1893→        *,
  1894→        init="k-means++",
  1895→        max_iter=100,
  1896→        batch_size=1024,
  1897→        verbose=0,
  1898→        compute_labels=True,
  1899→        random_state=None,
  1900→        tol=0.0,
  1901→        max_no_improvement=10,
  1902→        init_size=None,
  1903→        n_init="auto",
  1904→        reassignment_ratio=0.01,
  1905→    ):
  1906→        super().__init__(
  1907→            n_clusters=n_clusters,
  1908→            init=init,
  1909→            max_iter=max_iter,
  1910→            verbose=verbose,
  1911→            random_state=random_state,
  1912→            tol=tol,
  1913→            n_init=n_init,
  1914→        )
  1915→
  1916→        self.max_no_improvement = max_no_improvement
  1917→        self.batch_size = batch_size
  1918→        self.compute_labels = compute_labels
  1919→        self.init_size = init_size
  1920→        self.reassignment_ratio = reassignment_ratio
  1921→
  1922→    def _check_params_vs_input(self, X):
  1923→        super()._check_params_vs_input(X, default_n_init=3)
  1924→
  1925→        self._batch_size = min(self.batch_size, X.shape[0])
  1926→
  1927→        # init_size
  1928→        self._init_size = self.init_size
  1929→        if self._init_size is None:
  1930→            self._init_size = 3 * self._batch_size
  1931→            if self._init_size < self.n_clusters:
  1932→                self._init_size = 3 * self.n_clusters
  1933→        elif self._init_size < self.n_clusters:
  1934→            warnings.warn(
  1935→                (
  1936→                    f"init_size={self._init_size} should be larger than "
  1937→                    f"n_clusters={self.n_clusters}. Setting it to "
  1938→                    "min(3*n_clusters, n_samples)"
  1939→                ),
  1940→                RuntimeWarning,
  1941→                stacklevel=2,
  1942→            )
  1943→            self._init_size = 3 * self.n_clusters
  1944→        self._init_size = min(self._init_size, X.shape[0])
  1945→
  1946→        # reassignment_ratio
  1947→        if self.reassignment_ratio < 0:
  1948→            raise ValueError(
  1949→                "reassignment_ratio should be >= 0, got "
  1950→                f"{self.reassignment_ratio} instead."
  1951→            )
  1952→
  1953→    def _warn_mkl_vcomp(self, n_active_threads):
  1954→        """Warn when vcomp and mkl are both present"""
  1955→        warnings.warn(
  1956→            "MiniBatchKMeans is known to have a memory leak on "
  1957→            "Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than "
  1958→            "available threads. You can prevent it by setting "
  1959→            f"batch_size >= {self._n_threads * CHUNK_SIZE} or by "
  1960→            "setting the environment variable "
  1961→            f"OMP_NUM_THREADS={n_active_threads}"
  1962→        )
  1963→
  1964→    def _mini_batch_convergence(
  1965→        self, step, n_steps, n_samples, centers_squared_diff, batch_inertia
  1966→    ):
  1967→        """Helper function to encapsulate the early stopping logic"""
  1968→        # Normalize inertia to be able to compare values when
  1969→        # batch_size changes
  1970→        batch_inertia /= self._batch_size
  1971→
  1972→        # count steps starting from 1 for user friendly verbose mode.
  1973→        step = step + 1
  1974→
  1975→        # Ignore first iteration because it's inertia from initialization.
  1976→        if step == 1:
  1977→            if self.verbose:
  1978→                print(
  1979→                    f"Minibatch step {step}/{n_steps}: mean batch "
  1980→                    f"inertia: {batch_inertia}"
  1981→                )
  1982→            return False
  1983→
  1984→        # Compute an Exponentially Weighted Average of the inertia to
  1985→        # monitor the convergence while discarding minibatch-local stochastic
  1986→        # variability: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moving_average
  1987→        if self._ewa_inertia is None:
  1988→            self._ewa_inertia = batch_inertia
  1989→        else:
  1990→            alpha = self._batch_size * 2.0 / (n_samples + 1)
  1991→            alpha = min(alpha, 1)
  1992→            self._ewa_inertia = self._ewa_inertia * (1 - alpha) + batch_inertia * alpha
  1993→
  1994→        # Log progress to be able to monitor convergence
  1995→        if self.verbose:
  1996→            print(
  1997→                f"Minibatch step {step}/{n_steps}: mean batch inertia: "
  1998→                f"{batch_inertia}, ewa inertia: {self._ewa_inertia}"
  1999→            )
  2000→
  2001→        # Early stopping based on absolute tolerance on squared change of
  2002→        # centers position
  2003→        if self._tol > 0.0 and centers_squared_diff <= self._tol:
  2004→            if self.verbose:
  2005→                print(f"Converged (small centers change) at step {step}/{n_steps}")
  2006→            return True
  2007→
  2008→        # Early stopping heuristic due to lack of improvement on smoothed
  2009→        # inertia
  2010→        if self._ewa_inertia_min is None or self._ewa_inertia < self._ewa_inertia_min:
  2011→            self._no_improvement = 0

If `None`, all observations\n        are assigned equal weight. `sample_weight` is not used during\n        initialization if `init` is a callable or a user provided array.\n\n    init : {'k-means++', 'random'}, callable or array-like of shape \\\n            (n_clusters, n_features), default='k-means++'\n        Method for initialization:\n\n        - `'k-means++'` : selects initial cluster centers for k-mean\n          clustering in a smart way to speed up convergence. See section\n          Notes in k_init for more details.\n        - `'random'`: choose `n_clusters` observations (rows) at random from data\n          for the initial centroids.\n        - If an array is passed, it should be of shape `(n_clusters, n_features)`\n          and gives the initial centers.\n        - If a callable is passed, it should take arguments `X`, `n_clusters` and a\n          random state and return an initialization.\n\n    n_init : 'auto' or int, default=\"auto\"\n        Number of time the k-means algorithm will be run with different\n        centroid seeds. The final results will be the best output of\n        n_init consecutive runs in terms of inertia.\n\n        When `n_init='auto'`, the number of runs depends on the value of init:\n        10 if using `init='random'` or `init` is a callable;\n        1 if using `init='k-means++'` or `init` is an array-like.\n\n        .. versionadded:: 1.2\n           Added 'auto' option for `n_init`.\n\n        .. versionchanged:: 1.4\n           Default value for `n_init` changed to `'auto'`.\n\n    max_iter : int, default=300\n        Maximum number of iterations of the k-means algorithm to run.\n\n    verbose : bool, default=False\n        Verbosity mode.\n\n    tol : float, default=1e-4\n        Relative tolerance with regards to Frobenius norm of the difference\n        in the cluster centers of two consecutive iterations to declare\n        convergence.\n\n    random_state : int, RandomState instance or None, default=None\n        Determines random number generation for centroid initialization. Use\n        an int to make the randomness deterministic.\n        See :term:`Glossary <random_state>`.\n\n    copy_x : bool, default=True\n        When pre-computing distances it is more numerically accurate to center\n        the data first. If `copy_x` is True (default), then the original data is\n        not modified. However it's\n        more memory intensive due to the allocation of an extra array of shape\n        `(n_samples, n_clusters)`.\n\n        .. versionchanged:: 0.18\n            Added Elkan algorithm\n\n        .. versionchanged:: 1.1\n            Renamed \"full\" to \"lloyd\", and deprecated \"auto\" and \"full\".\n            Changed \"auto\" to use \"lloyd\" instead of \"elkan\".\n\n    return_n_iter : bool, default=False\n        Whether or not to return the number of iterations.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    centroid : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)\n        Centroids found at the last iteration of k-means.\n\n    label : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)\n        The `label[i]` is the code or index of the centroid the\n        i'th observation is closest to.\n\n    inertia : float\n        The final value of the inertia criterion (sum of squared distances to\n        the closest centroid for all observations in the training set).\n\n    best_n_iter : int\n        Number of iterations corresponding to the best results.\n        Returned only if `return_n_iter` is set to True.\n\n    Examples\n    --------\n    >>> import numpy as np\n    >>> from sklearn.cluster import k_means\n    >>> X = np.array([[1, 2], [1, 4], [1, 0],\n    ... X, n_clusters=2, n_init=\"auto\", random_state=0\n    ... )\n    >>> centroid\n    array([[10.,  2.],\n           [ 1.,  2.]])\n    >>> label\n    array([1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0], dtype=int32)\n    >>> inertia\n    16.0\n    \"\"\"\n    est = KMeans(\n        n_clusters=n_clusters,\n        init=init,\n        n_init=n_init,\n        max_iter=max_iter,\n        verbose=verbose,\n        tol=tol,\n        random_state=random_state,\n        copy_x=copy_x,\n        algorithm=algorithm,\n    ).fit(X, sample_weight=sample_weight)\n    if return_n_iter:\n        return est.cluster_centers_, est.labels_, est.inertia_, est.n_iter_\n    else:\n        return est.cluster_centers_, est.labels_, est.inertia_\n\n\ndef _kmeans_single_elkan(\n    X,\n    sample_weight,\n    centers_init,\n    max_iter=300,\n    verbose=False,\n    tol=1e-4,\n    n_threads=1,\n):\n    \"\"\"A single run of k-means elkan, assumes preparation completed prior.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    X : {ndarray, sparse matrix} of shape (n_samples, n_features)\n        The observations to cluster. Parallelism is\n        sample-wise on the main cython loop which assigns each sample to its\n        closest center.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    centroid : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features)\n        Centroids found at the last iteration of k-means.\n\n    label : ndarray of shape (n_samples,)\n        label[i] is the code or index of the centroid the\n        i'th observation is closest to.\n\n    inertia : float\n        The final value of the inertia criterion (sum of squared distances to\n        the closest centroid for all observations in the training set).\n\n    n_iter : int\n        Number of iterations run.\n    \"\"\"\n    n_samples = X.shape[0]\n    n_clusters = centers_init.shape[0]\n\n    # compute new pairwise distances between centers and closest other\n        # center of each center for next iterations\n        center_half_distances = euclidean_distances(centers_new) / 2\n        distance_next_center = np.partition(\n            np.asarray(center_half_distances), kth=1, axis=0\n        )[1]\n\n        if verbose:\n            inertia = _inertia(X, sample_weight, centers, labels, n_threads)\n            print(f\"Iteration {i}, inertia {inertia}\")\n\n        centers, centers_new = centers_new, centers\n\n        if np.array_equal(labels, labels_old):\n            # First check the labels for strict convergence.\n            if verbose:\n                print(f\"Converged at iteration {i}: strict convergence.\")\n            strict_convergence = True\n            break\n        else:\n            # = _tolerance(X, self.tol)\n\n        # n-init\n        if self.n_init == \"auto\":\n            if isinstance(self.init, str) and self.init == \"k-means++\":\n                self._n_init = 1\n cluster centers and predict cluster index for each sample.\n\n        Convenience method; equivalent to calling fit(X) followed by\n        predict(X).\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n
